findings.evidence: ./mathlib/.../SubMulAction.lean:435 `mem_orbit_subMul_iff {p} {x m : p}: x ∈ orbit R m ↔ (x:M) ∈ orbit R (m:M)`; Deck.lean:198 proof is `SubMulAction.mem_orbit_subMul_iff (p := fiberSubMulAction b)`.
findings.fix: Drop the lemma and use SubMulAction.mem_orbit_subMul_iff directly (fiberMulAction is defeq to (fiberSubMulAction b).mulAction), or justify why a named fibre wrapper is needed.
findings.issue: mem_fiber_orbit_iff is a pure specialization of SubMulAction.mem_orbit_subMul_iff; its proof is that lemma with (p := fiberSubMulAction b). It folds in nothing fibre-specific (no map_proj / fibre simplification).

findings.evidence: ./mathlib/.../SubMulAction.lean:461 `stabilizer_of_subMul {p} (m : p): stabilizer R m = stabilizer R (m:M)`; Deck.lean:204 proof is `SubMulAction.stabilizer_of_subMul (p := fiberSubMulAction b) e`.
findings.fix: Drop the lemma and apply SubMulAction.stabilizer_of_subMul directly, or justify the fibre-named wrapper.
findings.issue: fiber_stabilizer_eq is a pure specialization of SubMulAction.stabilizer_of_subMul; its proof is that lemma applied with (p := fiberSubMulAction b) e.

summary: Two lemmas verbatim-restate general Mathlib SubMulAction results specialized to the fibre subaction, adding only a name. The remainder is new material that reuses existing API correctly.
